canonicalRequest:
POST
https://api.openai.com/v1/responses
authorization: Bearer <SECRET>
content-type: application/json

{"input":[{"content":"You are an assistant embedded in a terminal app.

Tools:
    * Terminal: execute_command, search_command_history, get_command_history, create_file
    * (Others may be present.)

When to use tools:

1. If the user refers to terminal activity or asks to perform actions:
   - Commands to run: "run", "execute", "install", "update", "open [file]", "save", "create"
   - History queries: "commands I ran", "terminal history" 
   - Action verbs imply doing, not explaining: use execute_command
   - For searching history: use search_command_history
   - For retrieving history: use get_command_history
   
2. If the user asks general questions unrelated to terminal state, answer directly without tools.

Important: 
- Action words (install, run, execute, update, create, open, save) indicate the user wants you to DO something, not explain how.
- Never refuse to act because a tool might be unnecessary. Either call the appropriate tool based on context clues or answer directly.

After gathering evidence via tools, synthesize a clear answer. Be concise and helpful. If a zip file is provided (this is rare), you should extract it and analyze the contents in the context of the accompanying messages.","role":"system","type":"message"},{"content":"Write three paragraphs about the history of OpenSSL. Take your time and be thorough.","role":"user","type":"message"}],"max_output_tokens":124602,"model":"gpt-5.5","stream":true,"tool_choice":"auto","tools":[{"description":"Returns true if the terminal is at the command prompt, allowing safe command injection.","name":"is_at_prompt","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Runs a shell command and returns its output.","name":"execute_command","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"command":{"description":"The command to run","type":"string"}},"required":["command"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Retrieves the exit status of the last executed command.","name":"get_last_exit_status","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns the recent command history.","name":"get_command_history","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"limit":{"description":"Maximum number of history items to return.","type":"number"}},"required":["limit"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Retrieves the most recent command.","name":"get_last_command","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns the current partially typed command before the cursor.","name":"get_command_before_cursor","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Searches history for commands matching a query.","name":"search_command_history","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"query":{"description":"Search query for filtering command history.","type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns the output of a previous command by its unique identifier.","name":"get_command_output","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"id":{"description":"Unique identifier of the command whose output is requested.","type":"string"}},"required":["id"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns (columns, rows) of the terminal window.","name":"get_terminal_size","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Detects the shell in use (e.g., bash, fish, xonsh, zsh).","name":"get_shell_type","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns true if the user is SSH’ed into a remote hostname if in an SSH user’s username.","name":"get_user_identity","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns the current directory.","name":"get_current_directory","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Copies text to the clipboard.","name":"set_clipboard","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"text":{"description":"The text to copy to the clipboard.","type":"string"}},"required":["text"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Inserts text into the terminal input at the cursor position.","name":"insert_text_at_cursor","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"text":{"description":"The text to insert at the cursor position. Supports a small backslash-escape vocabulary so you can send control keys and special characters: \\ for a literal backslash, \n for newline, \r for carriage return, \t for tab, and \uXXXX (four hex digits, JSON-style) for any Unicode scalar. Examples: \u0004 for Ctrl-D / EOF, \u001a for Ctrl-Z, \u000c for Ctrl-L, \u001b for Escape. Consider whether execute_command would be a better choice, especially when running a command at the shell prompt since insert_text_at_cursor does not return the output to you.","type":"string"}},"required":["text"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Clears the current command line input (only at the prompt).","name":"delete_current_line","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"required":[],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Returns the content of a command's man page.","name":"get_man_page","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"cmd":{"description":"The command whose man page content is requested.","type":"string"}},"required":["cmd"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Creates a file containing a specified string on the user's computer and then reveals it in Finder.","name":"create_file","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"content":{"description":"The content that will be written to the file.","type":"string"},"filename":{"description":"The name of the file you wish to create. It will be replaced if it already exists.","type":"string"}},"required":["filename","content"],"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"description":"Ask the user to enable orchestration mode for this chat. Use this whenever the task requires interacting with the user's iTerm2 sessions and you don't currently have the tools to do it: sending text or keystrokes, running shell commands, reading on-screen output, controlling a running terminal program, watching a session for changes, or coordinating across multiple sessions (Code Review, Diff, Chat, etc.). This applies to single-session tasks as well as multi-session ones; if the user asks you to do something in their terminal and you have no way to act on a session, this is the tool to call. The user is prompted with a description of what orchestration grants and chooses to approve or decline. On approval, this chat switches to the orchestration tool surface (send_text, get_screen_contents, get_state, register_watch, etc.) starting with the next turn; the linked terminal or browser session, if any, is detached. Returns a string describing the outcome.","name":"request_orchestration_enable","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"strict":true,"type":"function"},{"container":{"file_ids":[],"type":"auto"},"type":"code_interpreter"}]}
